items.title: DBSCAN point classification
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  point["point p"]
  count["count neighbors within eps"]
  coreCheck{"|N_eps(p)| >= min_samples?"}
  core["core point: seeds cluster"]
  borderCheck{"within eps of a core point?"}
  border["border point: in cluster, no expansion"]
  noise["noise point: label -1"]
  point --> count --> coreCheck
  coreCheck -->|yes| core
  coreCheck -->|no| borderCheck
  borderCheck -->|yes| border
  borderCheck -->|no| noise
items.caption: Every point is classified as core, border, or noise before clusters grow from unvisited core seeds.

items.title: Cluster expansion by density reachability
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  coreA["core A"]
  coreB["core B"]
  borderQ["border Q"]
  noiseR["noise R"]
  coreA -->|"dist <= eps"| coreB
  coreA -->|"dist <= eps"| borderQ
  noiseR -.->|"too sparse"| noiseR
  clusterC["cluster C = density-connected via cores"]
  coreA --> clusterC
  coreB --> clusterC
  borderQ --> clusterC
items.caption: Clusters grow by chaining core points within eps; border points join but do not propagate; isolated points stay noise.

items.title: eps and min_samples interaction
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  largeEps["larger eps: neighborhoods merge"]
  smallEps["smaller eps: neighborhoods shrink"]
  highMin["higher min_samples: stricter cores"]
  lowMin["lower min_samples: easier cores"]
  fewClusters["fewer, broader clusters"]
  manyNoise["more noise labels"]
  largeEps --> fewClusters
  smallEps --> manyNoise
  highMin --> manyNoise
  lowMin --> fewClusters
items.caption: Tuning is two-dimensional: eps controls neighborhood size; min_samples controls how dense a region must be to seed growth.
